title: hoya wallichii subsp. wallichii
intro: Hoya wallichii subsp. wallichii is a rare and lesser-known species of the Hoya genus, commonly referred to as a climbing or twining plant known for its fragrant and star-shaped flowers. The plant is used in traditional medicine in various parts of Asia, where it is believed to possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The medicinal properties of Hoya wallichii subsp. wallichii are associated with several health benefits, including the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. The plant is traditionally prepared into herbal remedies such as infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which are used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
